scope_note: Rice does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff, an admitted-student GPA distribution, or any GPA breakdown by major. The cited application page focuses on official transcript and recommendation materials instead of a posted GPA threshold.
policy_summary: Rice does not state a minimum required GPA on its cited first-year application page. Rice instead requires official transcripts and recommendation letters, and frames academic readiness through holistic review rather than a published GPA cutoff.

what_mit_does_say.claim: Rice does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff for first-year applicants on its cited admissions page.
Rice requires official high school/secondary school transcripts with grades from 9th through 11th grade plus courses being taken in 12th grade.
Rice says the counselor and teacher recommendations should highlight the student’s accomplishments and academic strengths in the context of the high school and classroom.

recommended_gpa.summary: Rice does not publish HS GPA data for its incoming class. The numbers below are third-party estimates, not official Rice figures. Use them as directional references, not cutoffs.

estimates.label: Weighted (estimate)
estimates.method: Estimated using data from over 1,000 schools, because Rice did not officially report its average GPA.

estimates.label: Unweighted (prediction)
estimates.method: Predicted by a CollegeSimply algorithm using Rice's test scores and a large sample of college GPA data, because Rice has not published HS GPA data for incoming freshmen.

recommended_gpa.interpretation: Both estimates point to very high grades. The weighted 4.12 suggests many successful applicants combine A-range marks with advanced courses; the unweighted 3.96 is roughly an A/A- transcript.
